meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Passive Income to Fix Finishing Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn to build passive income SaaS products using Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly, high-impact guide.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project abandonment requires shifting from 'perfect' to 'profitable.' By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated iteration, you can launch micro-SaaS MVPs in hours. Focus on high-intent, low-maintenance utilities that solve specific pain points, ensuring recurring revenue without the perpetual grind of feature bloat.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and endless feature creep. As a founder, the inability to ship isn't a lack of talent; it is a failure of scope. You are building monuments to your ego rather than engines for your revenue. 

True passive income isn't about luck; it's about engineering constraints. By adopting a 'ship-fast' philosophy and utilizing modern AI-native development tools, you can transform your technical ADHD into a series of automated, cash-flowing assets. It is time to stop dreaming and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Utility
steps.description: Identify a specific, boring problem that users will pay $9/month to solve. Focus on single-function tools.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a list of 5 high-demand micro-SaaS ideas that solve a singular, repetitive task for content creators, requiring minimal maintenance after launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the market demand for a chrome extension that automates LinkedIn post scheduling and output a lean project roadmap.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the foundational boilerplate, ensuring your architecture is ready for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js 14 boilerplate with Supabase integration, Stripe subscription handling, and Tailwind CSS. Focus on a clean, modular structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project boilerplate generated by Antigravity and verify all environment variables are correctly mapped.'

steps.title: Automate Core Logic
steps.description: Implement the primary value driver. Keep the code lean to reduce future technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, efficient serverless function to process user input and trigger an external API call, including robust error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the serverless function logic for security vulnerabilities and optimize for cold-start performance.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments
steps.description: Connect Stripe to ensure your project is capable of generating revenue from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription status, user access tiers, and successful payment events in the Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Set up the Stripe webhook endpoint and test the subscription flow using the Stripe CLI.'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and set up automated monitoring to ensure the system remains passive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted SaaS, including automated uptime monitoring and database backup configurations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the Vercel deployment pipeline and add an automated health-check script for the primary API route.'

pro_tips: Always build for a specific audience; if you try to sell to everyone, you sell to no one.
Ignore 'perfect' code. If the MVP works and the Stripe webhook fires, ship it immediately.
Use AI to write your documentation as you code; it prevents the 'I don't know how this works' block later.
Set a 48-hour hard deadline for your MVP launch. If it takes longer, you are over-engineering.
Focus on one revenue stream (e.g., subscriptions) to keep your database and logic simple.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution and 20% on maintenance. Do not add new features until you have your first 10 paying customers.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop being a 'project starter' and become a 'product founder.' The only variable left is your execution. Stop overthinking and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
